statement: The restaurant currently operates entirely on manual processes using handwritten notebooks for all recording, with no inventory tracking system. This leads to untracked inventory loss and zero visibility into business performance.
root_cause: Absence of any digital system forces all processes to depend on human memory and manual recording, which is inherently unreliable — especially for busy multi-role staff who serve, cook, and handle payments simultaneously.
trigger: Owner's vision to fully digitalize restaurant operations to gain organized recording, management, and monitoring capabilities.
duration: Since business inception — manual process has been the only method used.

time.description: Time spent on manual order taking, handwritten recording, and end-of-day reconciliation

financial.description: Inventory items go missing regularly due to lack of tracking. Staff forget to record usage, resulting in untraceable financial leakage.

operational.description: No visibility into stock levels, sales performance, or operational efficiency. Owner cannot monitor business health in real-time.

pain_points: No queue or table management system
workarounds: Staff handles seating manually based on visual assessment

steps.description: Waiter takes customer order using handwritten notes
pain_points: Orders may be misread or lost
No digital record of what was ordered
workarounds: Waiter memorizes or verbally relays orders to kitchen

steps.description: Chef receives order and prepares food
pain_points: Verbal or handwritten orders may cause miscommunication
No tracking of ingredients used per order
workarounds: Chef asks waiter to clarify if order is unclear

pain_points: No system to track which orders are ready or served
workarounds: Chef calls out or waiter checks kitchen periodically

steps.description: Customer pays — waiter acts as cashier and calculates manually
pain_points: No sales record linked to specific orders
workarounds: Waiter uses calculator or mental math, writes total in notebook
